title: cyanea sessilifolia
intro: Cyanea sessilifolia, also known as the Hawaiian violet, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the islands of Hawaii. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and wounds. The health benefits of Cyanea sessilifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to reduce swelling and prevent infection. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin care.
